findings.title: Potential XSS via innerHTML
findings.description: Setting innerHTML with user input can lead to XSS vulnerabilities
findings.recommendation: Use textContent instead of innerHTML, or sanitize user input properly

findings.title: Hardcoded Secret
findings.description: Hardcoded secrets in source code pose security risks
findings.recommendation: Use environment variables or secure configuration management for secrets

findings.title: Weak Cryptographic Algorithm
findings.description: Use of weak or deprecated cryptographic algorithms
findings.recommendation: Use strong cryptographic algorithms like SHA-256, AES, or modern alternatives

findings.title: javascript.browser.security.insecure-document-method.insecure-document-method
findings.description: User controlled data in methods like `innerHTML`, `outerHTML` or `document.write` is an anti-pattern that can lead to XSS vulnerabilities
findings.recommendation: Review and fix the issue identified by Semgrep rule: javascript.browser.security.insecure-document-method.insecure-document-method
